name: #switching classes on G == 2^{β_1(G)}
hypothesis: any G; wrap subsets modulo W ~ W △ δ(S)

name: parity of |W ∩ γ| on each cycle γ is switching invariant
hypothesis: for any W, S ⊆ V, every cycle γ: |W∩γ| ≡ |(W△δS)∩γ| mod 2

name: G balanced iff every cycle γ has |W ∩ γ| even
hypothesis: G connected; check equivalence of balance and global cycle-even

name: switching-class → (parity on each basis cycle) is a bijection
hypothesis: G connected; basis cycles γ₁,...,γ_m; map W ↦ (|W∩γ_i| mod 2)_i

name: # ε witnessing balance == 2^{#π₀(G)} when all comps balanced, else 0
hypothesis: if any component unbalanced, count = 0; else 2^{#π₀}

name: bal_radius(G,W) == min_S |W △ δ(S)|
hypothesis: any G,W

name: max_W bal_radius(G,W) ≤ |E| / 2  (rounded-up)
hypothesis: connected G, any W

name: bridge-gluing: (G1 ⊔ G2 + bridge e, W) balanced iff each side balanced
hypothesis: G1,G2 small; new bridge connects v1∈V(G1) and v2∈V(G2); e may or may not be wrap

name: vertex-identification gluing preserves balance iff each side balanced
hypothesis: identify v1∈V(G1) with v2∈V(G2), keep wraps

name: kernel_dim(L_möb) is a switching invariant
hypothesis: kernel_dim(L_möb(G,W)) == kernel_dim(L_möb(G,W△δS)) for any S

name: W is a coboundary iff every cycle has even intersection with W
hypothesis: any G,W

name: β(G,W) == β(G, W△δ(S))
hypothesis: any G,W and S ⊆ V

name: #coboundaries = 2^{|V|−#π₀}; # cocycle classes = 2^{β_1}
hypothesis: any G; cocycle class = switching class of wrap; coboundary = δ(S)

name: |V|−|E| == #π₀(G) − β_1(G)
hypothesis: Euler characteristic χ(G) for any graph

name: dim ker(L_signed(G,W)) == β(G,W)
hypothesis: any G,W; signed Laplacian = D − A_W

name: rank(L_signed(G,W)) == |V| − β(G,W)
hypothesis: any G,W

name: distinct switching classes have min_S |W△W'△δ(S)| ≥ 1
hypothesis: trivial separator lemma

name: #switching classes(G) = ∏_i 2^{β_1(C_i)}
hypothesis: G with possibly multiple components

name: bipartite G: bal_radius(G,W) ≤ min(|W|, |E|−|W|)
hypothesis: G bipartite (∅ and E are both coboundaries)

name: ker(L_signed) on balanced connected G matches ε-partition
hypothesis: G connected, balanced; ker vector v has sign = (−1)^{ε(u)} up to global sign

name: #coboundaries · #switching-classes == 2^|E|
hypothesis: short exact sequence of Z/2 cochain complex
